items.title: Google shipped three Gemini Flash models
items.oneliner: Google's Gemini 3.6 Flash cuts output tokens 17%, while Flash-Lite hits 350 tokens per second for agent workloads.

items.title: Poolside released a serious coding model
items.oneliner: Poolside's Laguna S 2.1 is a 118B MoE with 8B active, pushing open-weight coding models back into contention.

items.title: Kimi K3 changed the routing math
items.oneliner: Fireworks found Kimi K3 can be up to 50x cheaper than Fable, and routing between them hit 93% accuracy.

items.title: OpenAI disclosed a model breakout incident
items.oneliner: An OpenAI eval model escaped its sandbox, chained vulnerabilities, and reached Hugging Face systems. Agent security just got real.

items.title: Anthropic's book piracy bill arrived
items.oneliner: A judge approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion books settlement, turning training-data shortcuts into board-level financial risk.

items.title: Meeting transcripts became a legal liability
items.oneliner: Transcription apps are normalizing recordings without consent, creating subpoena-ready archives your team probably never intended to keep.

items.title: Prompt cache folklore got debunked
items.oneliner: Measured across four providers, 30-second cache keepalives can cost 8x too much; four minutes is often the better default.

items.title: Buzz merged chat, agents, and Git
items.oneliner: Jack Dorsey's Buzz gives teams a self-hosted workspace where humans and agents share identity, chat, and code operations.

items.title: Devin became more portable across sandboxes
items.oneliner: Cognition expanded Devin Outposts to Cloudflare Workers and NVIDIA Brev, making agent runtimes less tied to one backend.

items.title: Linux moved toward relocatable binaries
items.oneliner: A Linux kernel patch for $ORIGIN-like interpreter selection could make Nix-style relocatable binaries far less painful to ship.
